title: PCI-DSS 8.4 — MFA coverage: administrative access to CDE without MFA
exclusions: Does NOT govern who can be an admin; detects admin access attempts into the CDE that did not use MFA.
evidence: Saved search 'pci_8_4_mfa_coverage' (every 10m) emits per session: admin, dest, session_type, mfa_used, to compliance_summary.

compliance.assurance_rationale: PCI-DSS 8.4 MFA for administrative access to the CDE — directly evidenced.
ISO/IEC 27001:2022 A.8.5 Secure authentication — directly evidenced.
NIST 800-53 IA-2(1) MFA to privileged accounts — directly evidenced.

controlTest.positiveScenario: A test admin session from bastion to a CDE host with mfa_used=0 fires within 10m.
controlTest.negativeScenario: A bastion session showing mfa_used=1 is absent.

dataSources: `index=windows` EventCode 4624/4672; `index=linux` auditd; bastion/PAM `index=pam` sourcetype=cyberark:pam; CDE-dest lookup `cde_hosts.csv`.
spl:
search (index=windows sourcetype=WinEventLog:Security EventCode IN (4624,4672)) OR (index=pam sourcetype=cyberark:pam event_type=session_start) earliest=-10m
| rename target_host AS dest
| lookup cde_hosts.csv hostname=dest OUTPUT in_cde
| lookup privileged_accounts.csv user AS admin OUTPUT is_privileged
| where in_cde="yes" AND is_privileged="yes"
| eval mfa_used=if(match(_raw,"(?i)mfa|2fa|push|webauthn|smartcard"),1,0)
| where mfa_used=0
| table admin dest session_type mfa_used
description: Administrative access to the CDE must use MFA. PCI-DSS 8.4 (formerly 8.3) explicitly requires MFA for administrative access. This UC detects misses.
value: The #1 QSA finding in failed assessments. Converting this to a 10-minute detection reduces remediation cost 100×.
grandmaExplanation: The #1 QSA finding in failed assessments — so you can show exactly what is being watched and when something went wrong.
implementation: (1) Integrate PAM/bastion; (2) maintain cde_hosts.csv + privileged_accounts.csv; (3) schedule UC every 10m; (4) hit revokes session; (5) monthly CISO review.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for CyberArk.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=windows` EventCode 4624/4672; `index=linux` auditd; bastion/PAM `index=pam` sourcetype=cyberark:pam; CDE-dest lookup `cde_hosts.csv`..
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Integrate PAM/bastion; (2) maintain cde_hosts.csv + privileged_accounts.csv; (3) schedule UC every 10m; (4) hit revokes session; (5) monthly CISO review.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
search (index=windows sourcetype=WinEventLog:Security EventCode IN (4624,4672)) OR (index=pam sourcetype=cyberark:pam event_type=session_start) earliest=-10m
| rename target_host AS dest
| lookup cde_hosts.csv hostname=dest OUTPUT in_cde
| lookup privileged_accounts.csv user AS admin OUTPUT is_privileged
| where in_cde="yes" AND is_privileged="yes"
| eval mfa_used=if(match(_raw,"(?i)mfa|2fa|push|webauthn|smartcard"),1,0)
| where mfa_used=0
| table admin dest session_type mfa_used
```

Understanding this SPL

**PCI-DSS 8.4 — MFA coverage: administrative access to CDE without MFA** — The #1 QSA finding in failed assessments. Converting this to a 10-minute detection reduces remediation cost 100×.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=windows` EventCode 4624/4672; `index=linux` auditd; bastion/PAM `index=pam` sourcetype=cyberark:pam; CDE-dest lookup `cde_hosts.csv`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for CyberArk. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: windows, pam; **sourcetype**: WinEventLog:Security, cyberark:pam. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Applies an explicit `search` filter to narrow the current result set.
• Renames fields with `rename` for clarity or joins.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where in_cde="yes" AND is_privileged="yes"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **mfa_used** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where mfa_used=0` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **PCI-DSS 8.4 — MFA coverage: administrative access to CDE without MFA**): table admin dest session_type mfa_used


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Heatmap of admins × destinations, table of non-MFA sessions, single value 'non-MFA admin in CDE'.
visualization: Heatmap of admins × destinations, table of non-MFA sessions, single value 'non-MFA admin in CDE'.
knownFalsePositives: Break-glass emergency access with compensating controls (hardware-token-only) must log mfa_used=1 via its own sourcetype — route to the field correctly.
